feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.content: A roundup of recent reviews published by Engadget.

entry.title: Your Samsung Gallery won't be able to sync with Microsoft OneDrive soon
entry.content: Your photos don't have to go home, but they can't stay here.

entry.title: Cardinals rookie Jimmy Crooks makes strong case to stay in St. Louis after latest opportunity
entry.content: A big blast last night.

entry.title: Conor McGregor's timeline of trouble, from Khabib Nurmagomedov bus incident to 2024 sexual assault case
entry.content: As Conor McGregor makes his return to the octagon, plenty of focus is on his personal troubles.

entry.title: World Cup bracket 2026: Live updated FIFA knockout stages and printable version for download
entry.content: Download The Sporting News’ printable 2026 World Cup bracket, including expert tips and predictions to help pick every game in the quarterfinals.

entry.title: Bluesky has an official CEO again
entry.content: Toni Schneider, who took over after Jay Graber stepped down, is now Bluesky's official CEO.

entry.title: Where to watch Argentina vs. Switzerland live stream, TV channel, start time for World Cup quarterfinal match
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know to watch the Argentina vs. Switzerland World Cup quarterfinal match, including where to watch on TV or live stream, what time the game starts and more.

entry.title: Women's Wimbledon final live tennis score, results, highlights from Karolina Muchova vs. Linda Noskova
entry.content: Wimbledon scores: The Sporting News is bringing you live updates from the women's final on Centre Court as Karolina Muchova and Linda Noskova battle it out for a maiden grand slam title.

entry.title: Roy Keane vs. Erling Haaland beef, explained: Revisiting infamous dirty tackle on dad in 2001 Manchester Derby
entry.content: Here's a comprehensive look at Roy Keane's relationship with Erling Haaland and his father, former Manchester City midfielder Alf-Inge Haaland.

entry.title: Lakers, Peyton Watson might've just been given a fun hint at free agent partnership
entry.content: Los Angeles would certainly boost its roster with Watson.

entry.title: Sophie Cunningham, Lexie Hull relax in bikinis at iconic Las Vegas beach club
entry.content: Their second stop in Vegas this week.

entry.title: Engadget Indie Pitch: Penguin Colony
entry.content: "You won't know until you do and then it is too late."

entry.title: As gas plants rise to power AI, renewable energy allies are fighting for cleaner alternatives
entry.content: Renewable energy allies are trying to ensure that massive data centers will be powered by climate-friendly sources

entry.title: Four NYT journalists subpoenaed days after report questioned Air Force One security
entry.content: The Trump administration has subpoenaed several New York Times journalists after their report on security concerns involving the new Air Force One, according to the paper.

entry.title: Who needs scalpers when GameStop is marking up Pokémon cards by more than 300 percent?
entry.content: Is paying a reasonable price for colorful cardboard too much to ask?

entry.title: Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara
entry.content: Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents sayNigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official.Government troops targeted the gangs in Gummi district in a two-day operation that “led to the elimination of more than 300 terrorists”, Zamfara’s information commissioner, Mahmud Muhammad Dantawasa, said in a statement. Continue reading...

entry.title: Palworld 1.0, Tiny Bookshop on mobile and other new indie games worth checking out
entry.content: Our weekly roundup of the best new indie games is here.

entry.title: Barcelona close in on signing 18-year-old Belgian pearl after €10m offer tabled
entry.content: Barcelona scouted and signed Roony Bardghji last summer amid much hype, stealing the winger’s signing at a bargain fee as Lamine Yamal’s backup.Unfortunately for him, however, the Catalans did not...

entry.title: 'I hugged him tightly' - Courtois backs Lammens, wants Belgium break
entry.content: Belgium goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois still had a job to do after being substituted with injury in Friday's World Cup quarter-final defeat to Spain - console his replacement Senne Lammens. The Manchester United keeper, who had an impressive first season in England, spilled a long-range shot from Pau Cubarsi on 88 minutes and allowed Mikel Merino to grab Spain's late winner for 2-1.

entry.title: Barcelona World Cup Spotlight: Cubarsi, Lamine Yamal impress for Spain vs Belgium
entry.content: The second quarter-final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup took place last night, with Spain facing Belgium.Both teams knew that a win would secure their place in the semi-finals, where France await, and La ...

entry.title: Real Madrid World Cup Spotlight: Cucurella progresses while Courtois suffers an injury
entry.content: The problem with Real Madrid players facing one another in World Cup games is that one of them is bound to go out at the end of the night.Last night, Spain and Belgium faced off in the quarter-finals ...

entry.title: 🎥 Mad, these Norwegians: best rowing video of this World Cup!
entry.content: The thousands of Norwegians rowing are THE standout fan highlight of this World Cup.Whether in Times Square, Boston, or stadiums all across North America, the Scandinavian supporters are basically set...

entry.title: Tuchel reveals positive injury update ahead of Norway showdown
entry.content: Thomas Tuchel says Reece James will be back available for England’s quarter-final tie with Norway.The defender has missed the last three games because of a hamstring injury but returned to team trai...

entry.title: Liverpool have found their next Fernando Torres in ex-Manchester City ace
entry.content: Fernando Torres is without doubt one of the best strikers to grace Liverpool with his presence in their recent history. El Nino, as he was known, was one of the main reasons why the Reds were feared d...

entry.title: PREVIEW: England look to stop Haaland and reach final four against Norway
entry.content: England head to Miami today to take on Norway in the FIFA World Cup quarter-final, as they look to reach the final four for just the fourth time in history.So far, England have topped the group ahead ...

entry.title: Argentina vs. Switzerland history: Head-to-head matches at World Cup, last meeting, team records and more
entry.content: Here's the rundown on how many times Argentina and Switzerland have shared the pitch.

entry.title: What does Braut Haaland mean? Explaining Erling Haaland's full name on back of jersey
entry.content: Here's why the Norwegian star has Braut Haaland on the back of his jersey at the World Cup.

entry.title: England vs. Norway history: Head-to-head matches at World Cup, last meeting, team records and more
entry.content: Norway are hoping to cause England a humiliation on the big stage this weekend. It would not be the first time.

entry.title: Who is Dan Burn? Meet England's 6-7 defender who could be key to stopping Erling Haaland amid World Cup injuries
entry.content: England will know first-hand how difficult the task will be in stopping superstar Norway striker Erling Haaland when the two clash in the 2026 FIFA World Cup quarterfinals on Saturday.

entry.title: Erling Haaland family tree: Meet soccer-playing dad, mom, sister and more about England-Norway roots
entry.content: Erling Haaland isn't the first member of his family to play in the World Cup. Here's more to know about the Norway star's family.

entry.title: Norway vs. England projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 quarterfinal game in Miami
entry.content: A highly intriguing all-European matchup graces the 2026 FIFA World Cup quarterfinals in Miami on Saturday as beaten Euro 2024 finalists England take on Norway.

entry.title: Erling Haaland and Jude Bellingham on same team: Revisiting the bromance from 2020-22 Borussia Dortmund
entry.content: Here's a look at Erling Haaland and Jude Bellingham's friendship, a relationship expected to take center stage when Norway battles England in the World Cup quarterfinal.

entry.title: Conor McGregor 'billionaire strut', explained: How UFC star popularized the swagger walk
entry.content: Here's the rundown on McGregor's famous strut, which may or may not make an appearance on his return.

entry.title: Matt Olson plays in 741st straight game with Braves to become the franchise's Iron Man
entry.content: Atlanta Braves first baseman Matt Olson made franchise history on Friday night against the St. Louis Cardinals when he played in his 741st consecutive game for the team to break Dale Murphy’s record of 740 set from 1981 to 1986. Olson signed with Atlanta as a free agent ahead of the 2022 season after playing parts of six seasons with the Oakland Athletics. “It’s quite an accomplishment,” Braves manager Walt Weiss said.

entry.title: England’s tortured relationship with quarter-finals is over – but beating Norway would break new ground
entry.content: England shattered their old glass ceiling with successive deep runs under Gareth Southgate, writes Richard Jolly, and are back at this telling stage with Thomas Tuchel at the 2026 World Cup

entry.title: Highest successful run chases in all T20 cricket at Rose Bowl, Southampton: Full list of records including T20Is
entry.content: With India and England both getting ready for the fifth and final T20I of a five-match fiasco, here's a look at how teams batting second have fared at the iconic venue

entry.title: Conor McGregor nickname, explained: The story behind 'The Notorious' and how it started
entry.content: Here's the background to the Irish star's famous nickname.

entry.title: Conor McGregor walkout song: Inside UFC star's famous 'The Foggy Dew'-'Hypnotize' remix
entry.content: Here's a look at the tune McGregor plays in the background while walking out to the cage.

entry.title: Barcelona set medical and presentation date for new signing Karim Adeyemi – report
entry.content: Barcelona are now putting the finishing touches on one of their biggest moves of the summer, with Karim Adeyemi’s arrival in Catalonia entering its final phase. After reaching a full agreement with...

entry.title: Who could take a penalty for England if World Cup quarter-final against Norway goes to a shootout?
entry.content: England have won only one penalty shootout at a World Cup before but Thomas Tuchel predicts they will need to win two to triumph in North America this summer

entry.title: Cricket royalty at Wimbledon: Sachin Tendulkar, Brian Lara to Shubman Gill grace Royal Box, India captain shares moment with Roger Federer
entry.content: India captain Shubman Gill took the internet by storm after meeting Roger Federer at Wimbledon 2026, joining Sachin Tendulkar in the exclusive Royal Box.

entry.title: Kirst-Sowers duo powers Waterdogs past Redwoods in statement win
entry.content: The Philadelphia Waterdogs’ thrilling 15-13 victory over the California Redwoods on Friday might have been the Premier Lacrosse League’s game of the year so far. The bigger story, though, was...

entry.title: Wildin’! Conor McGregor shows up in lifts with neck hickies for UFC 329 ceremonial weigh-ins
entry.content: There’s nothing like a Conor McGregor ceremonial weigh-in, and UFC 329 was extra special given it was McGregor’s first in five years. McGregor is set to face Max Holloway for the second time after beating “Blessed” in dominant fashion 13 years ago. A lot has changed since then. Holloway went on to be a dominant […]

entry.title: Report: Former Gonzaga guard Malachi Smith waived by Brooklyn Nets
entry.content: Jul. 10—Malachi Smith was rock-solid for the Brooklyn Nets during a 15-game stint to close the 2025-26 NBA season, but the former Gonzaga guard won't be on the team's roster next season after being waived Friday. The Nets exercised Smith's minimum-salary team option at the end of June, but elected to move on from the 26-year-old who spent the majority of the season with Brooklyn's G League ...

entry.title: Timing of Heat video with Bam Adebayo truly wild with Tyler Herro fight news
entry.content: Whew, this is something else.

entry.title: Braves at Cardinals rain delay chat and discussion
entry.content: The Braves and Cardinals are playing in St. Louis, so of course there’s a rain delay.

entry.title: Samuel Basallo leads Orioles to victory with 8th inning homer
entry.content: Nothing like a late-inning comeback to kick off the weekend

entry.title: Dodgers vs. D-backs game chat
entry.content: The Dodgers (61-33) take on the D-backs (46-47) at Dodger Stadium Friday night for a NL West clash. Shohei Ohtani was scratched from his start due to continued left knee irritation. Kyle Hurt will open a bullpen game for the Dodgers in the series opener. Eduardo Rodriguez (7-3, 2.25 ERA, 1.16 WHIP) goes for the […]

entry.title: Arthur Fery's Cinderella run ends in Wimbledon semifinal match to Alexander Zverev
entry.content: Championship weekend at Wimbledon has arrived, but it will not include the "Fery-tale" British matchup that tennis fans had been hoping for. Matthew Futterman, senior writer for tennis at The Athletic, joins "The Daily Report" to preview the gentlemen's singles final.

entry.title: Judge vacates convictions of 4 Proud Boys in Jan. 6 Capitol insurrection
entry.content: A federal judge on Friday agreed to dismiss the convictions of four members of the far-right Proud Boys group for their actions in the Jan. 6 Capitol riot.

entry.title: How Congress' housing bill may, or may not, impact Americans
entry.content: A sweeping bipartisan housing affordability bill is set to go into law at midnight on Friday, without President Trump's signature. The president says his decision not to sign the bill is a protest against lawmakers' failure to pass his package of voting reforms and restrictions. Bruce Marks, founder and CEO of the Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

entry.title: Former CIA officer breaks down the latest with Iran
entry.content: Senior U.S. officials say Iran privately told mediators that it made a mistake in shooting at commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The officials say Iran blamed the attacks on hardliners who are trying to undermine negotiations. Elliot Ackerman, a CBS News contributor and a former CIA officer, has more.

entry.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for July 12, 2026
entry.content: This week's guests include White House border czar Tom Homan and retired Gen. Frank McKenzie, president of The Citadel and former commander of U.S. Central Command.

entry.title: Hegseth frustrated with lack of adherence to grooming rules
entry.content: Eight months ago, Hegseth told top military leaders there would be "no more beardos" and "fat troops."

entry.title: A boy's simple habit of waving to his neighbors transformed his community
entry.content: Roman Butzlaff brought together a group of neighbors who say they would have barely known each other if not for a little boy, who lived in a neighborhood but needed a village.

entry.title: What stands out from latest Pentagon UFO files release, according to Disclosure Foundation director
entry.content: The Pentagon on Friday released a new batch of UFO files, spanning 19 videos and more. Jordan Flowers, executive director of the Disclosure Foundation, joins CBS News to discuss.

entry.title: 4-year-old heals "inner loneliness" by bringing his neighborhood together
entry.content: The old adage "it takes a village to raise a child" was turned on its head when it took a child to unite a village in North Carolina. Steve Hartman goes "On the Road" with the story.

entry.title: Detainees say ICE officer shot Houston driver through passenger window, lawyer says
entry.content: Three men who witnessed a fatal shooting involving federal immigration officers in Houston say no officer was threatened, a lawyer who has spoken with them said.

entry.title: Trump again says Iran ceasefire is over, but U.S. will keep talking
entry.content: Efforts are underway to get U.S.-Iran peace talks back on track after the most intense exchange of attacks since the ceasefire took effect.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Be true to your work, your word, and your friend.
Lucky Numbers: 86, 66, 56, 75, 57, 60

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Put a stocking over the end of a vacuum to find tiny items, like earrings.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Eskimos have over 15 words for the English word of 'Snow'

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Ray Bradbury
entry.content: “Stuff your eyes with wonder... live as if you'd drop dead in ten seconds. See the world. It's more fantastic than any dream made or paid for in factories.” — Ray Bradbury

entry.title: Samuel Alito, a MAGA-Friendly Justice With a New Conservative Legal Vision
entry.content: Samuel Alito is fusing establishment conservatism and the Trump movement into a new legal doctrine. His approach to the law may outlast them both.

entry.title: Yale Faces Rising Pressure to Resist a Settlement With the Trump Administration
entry.content: Sen. Richard Blumenthal, a Yale alum, has joined a growing chorus of faculty and students urging the university to resist making a settlement.

entry.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 12)
entry.content: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

entry.title: Cyclosporiasis parasite cases found in 31 states
entry.content: Cases of cyclosporiasis, a disease caused by a microscopic parasite, are growing in 31 states, and health officials still haven't identified the exact source of the outbreak. Dr. Eric Ascher, a family medicine physician, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss the illness.

entry.title: Trump admin. to back tariffs on Russian oil in effort to end Ukraine war, sources say
entry.content: The war in Ukraine is now in its fifth year. A bipartisan bill would impose heavy financial penalties on purchasers of Russian oil,

entry.title: Parents of teen found dead on island don't believe he chose to stay behind
entry.content: Christine and Elmore Wonsley said they don't believe their son would've stayed on the island when his friends left by boat.

entry.title: Apple sues OpenAI, accusing ChatGPT maker of stealing trade secrets
entry.content: Apple alleges that OpenAI and two of its employees stole trade secrets and engaged in a "pattern of misconduct."

entry.title: 'Dream come true' for longtime Ohio star playing first pro event at Firestone
entry.content: Summit County native Ryan Armour plays his first pro tournament at Firestone as the event prepares to leave Ohio.

entry.title: Bam Adebayo punched Tyler Herro over social media criticism in Las Vegas
entry.content: Bam Adebayo and Tyler Herro were teammates on the Miami Heat for seven seasons before the team traded Herro to the Milwaukee Bucks last month as part of the massive Giannis Antetokounmpo deal. Herro reportedly shared some critical comments about Adebayo after the trade on social media, and the star big man didn’t like it. […]

entry.title: Zach McKinstry looking closer to All-Star self for Detroit Tigers
entry.content: Detroit Tigers utility player Zach McKinstry has been surging on offense right as the Tigers have racked up needed wins in July.

entry.title: DFB execs in talks with Jürgen Klopp in New York
entry.content: Keep an eye out for a guy with great teeth talking to a group of stuffy businessmen.

entry.title: Mariners Game #95 Preview and Discussion, 7/10/26: SEA vs TBR
entry.content: Last year at about this time, the Mariners got stomped by the Yankees and looked ready to limp into the All-Star break with a final series against the best team in baseball. Then they went to Detroit and beat the pants off the Tigers, setting up one of the most important second halves in team […]

entry.title: Cowboys 2026 roster rundown: TE Michael Trigg
entry.content: Michael Trigg is going to be an interesting name to watch at training camp.

entry.title: Opinion: Why I think Arsenal’s latest signing is a no-brainer
entry.content: Arsenal’s latest summer signing has generated mixed reactions among supporters, with some questioning whether Illan Meslier is the right goalkeeper to strengthen Mikel Arteta’s squad.Dan Smith, ho...

entry.title: Spencer Steer to start in CF as Reds open series against Cubs
entry.content: What in the world are the Cincinnati Reds doing now?

entry.title: U.S. and Iran pause fighting after 2 days of intense strikes
entry.content: President Trump again said on Friday that the ceasefire with Iran is "over," but that the U.S. will continue negotiating. CBS News' Margaret Brennan and Elliot Ackerman have the latest.

entry.title: Maine Senate candidate on push to unseat Susan Collins, Platner replacement process
entry.content: Graham Platner is officially out of the Maine Senate race after facing sexual assault allegations. Democratic candidate Dan Kleban joins CBS News to make his case as Platner's replacement.

entry.title: HHS compiling list of injuries eligible for automatic COVID vaccine compensation
entry.content: The Health and Human Services department is compiling a list of injuries that would make COVID vaccine recipients automatically eligible for compensation. Dr. Céline Gounder joins The Takeout to explain what this means.

entry.title: Graham Platner formally drops out of Maine Senate race
entry.content: Graham Platner had until 5 p.m. on Monday to formally suspend his campaign or he would remain on the ballot in November.

entry.title: Landmark bipartisan housing bill to become law without Trump's signature
entry.content: The landmark bipartisan housing bill is set to become law overnight into Saturday despite President Trump's refusal to sign it. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes reports.

entry.title: Apple calls OpenAI's hardware business 'rotten to its core' in trade secret theft lawsuit
entry.content: The lawsuit also names io Products, the hardware company led by Jony Ive.

entry.title: Watch: Pentagon releases 19 new UFO videos
entry.content: The Defense Department released a fourth batch of UFO files on Friday, nearly one month after its third drop. These are all of the videos in the latest tranche, plus analysis from astrophysicist Avi Loeb.

entry.title: Mamdani announces new Click-to-Cancel rule for New York City
entry.content: The rule revives a proposed FTC protection that was abandoned last year.

entry.title: Marlins reportedly want to keep pitcher Sandy Alcantara, add pieces at MLB trade deadline
entry.content: The Marlins have played themselves into playoff contention in June and July, and are looking to add players as a result.

entry.title: Tearful Thibaut Courtois forced off before Senne Lammens error costs Belgium in World Cup quarter-final
entry.content: Courtois came off after picking up a leg problem and was replaced by Manchester United’s Senne Lammens

entry.title: What happened to Thibaut Courtois? Why Begium goalie left game vs. Spain and who replaced him
entry.content: Here's what we know about Thibaut Courtois' premature exit in Belgium's quarterfinals matchup against Spain.

entry.title: Mavericks front office looks genius after their top pick puts on a show against Warriors
entry.content: Morez Johnson Jr. silences draft critics by dominating his Dallas Mavericks Summer League debut against the Warriors alongside teammate Cooper Flagg.

entry.title: Thibaut Courtois injury update: Why Belgium goalkeeper left vs. Spain
entry.content: Belgium suffered a major blow late in its World Cup quarterfinal game against Spain as goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois exited with an injury.

entry.title: Manchester United are now interested in Wolves midfielder Joao Gomes after Ederson blow
entry.content: Manchester United are keen on a move for Wolverhampton Wanderers midfielder Joao Gomes after pulling out of a deal for Ederson, according to Brazilian outlet GE Globo. The Red Devils agreed a deal wit...

entry.title: Hynix Soars, Chips Stay Grounded
entry.content: Plus, oil retreats again and Delta sales rise

entry.title: Fears Over New Luxury Jet Forced Trump Back to Air Force One After Israeli Warning
entry.content: Worries the president’s new plane lacked defensive capabilities, combined with an Israeli warning about a possible threat, led him to fly on an old Air Force One.

entry.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Delta, Circle, Vodafone, Intel and more
entry.content: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

entry.title: County seeks help to care for 16 siblings from squalid home, prosecute their family
entry.content: The discovery of 16 siblings who authorities say were held at a home for years in squalid conditions is straining their rural Ohio county’s resources as it works to prosecute their parents and two grandparents and address the needs of so many children ...

entry.title: Iran Is Rushing Its Own Ships Through a Snarled Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: Since fighting restarted this week, traffic has largely stalled. But not for Tehran.

entry.title: Red Sox plane issues reportedly leave team stuck in Chicago hours before game in New York
entry.content: The Red Sox are still planning to play Friday night.

entry.title: Superb Sinner ends Djokovic's latest bid for history
entry.content: Jannik Sinner produces a devastating display to end Novak Djokovic's latest bid for history and set up a Wimbledon final against Alexander Zverev.

entry.title: Veteran Phillies slugger Kyle Schwarber joins teammate Bryce Harper in Home Run Derby
entry.content: Veteran Philadelphia Phillies slugger Kyle Schwarber is the latest participant announced for the Home Run Derby in Philadelphia on Monday night. Schwarber joins his teammate Bryce Harper in the eight-man field and the pair will try to put on show for the home crowd at Citizens Bank Park. Boston’s Willson Contreras, Kansas City’s Jac Caglianone, Tampa Bay’s Junior Caminero, St. Louis' Jordan Walker and the New York Yankees’ Ben Rice will also be part of the competition.
